resources.title: Enterprise AI Agent Eval Checklist
resources.description: A practical checklist for platform, security, and product leaders deciding whether an agent is ready for production.
resources.audience: Platform leads, AI product owners, security reviewers

sections.title: Workload Definition
items: Name the business workflow, user persona, risk level, and owner.
Convert real incidents, support tickets, or repo tasks into a versioned challenge pack.
Separate must-pass cases from exploratory cases before the first run.

sections.title: Baseline And Candidate
items: Record the current production agent, model alias, tool policy, and harness version as the baseline.
Run candidates against the same workload, tools, time budget, and retry policy.
Compare against baseline on correctness, cost, latency, and evidence completeness.

sections.title: Evidence And Review
items: Require replay links, scorecards, artifacts, and redacted traces for release review.
Label evidence tier when vendor agents or hosted tools hide trajectory details.
Keep a named reviewer for platform, security, product, and finance signoff.

sections.title: Gate Policy
items: Block on correctness regression, policy violation, unsafe tool use, or hard cost and latency ceilings.
Warn on unstable exploratory metrics while the benchmark matures.
Promote every production failure into the challenge pack within one sprint.

resources.title: Agent Release Gate Worksheet
resources.description: A one-page worksheet for translating eval results into ship, warn, or block rules your CI pipeline can enforce.

sections.title: Decision Inputs
items: Baseline run ID and refresh rule.
Candidate deployment, model alias, tool policy, and sandbox image.
Challenge pack version and owner.

sections.title: Thresholds
items: Correctness regression budget by challenge family.
Maximum cost per successful task.
Latency and time-to-first-token ceilings for user-facing paths.

sections.title: Automatic Blocks
items: Security policy violation or unapproved tool call.
Missing replay evidence for a critical task.
Malformed final output in a schema-bound workflow.

sections.title: CI Handoff
items: Attach gate JSON, scorecard JSON, and replay links to every release PR.
Document the exception owner, expiry date, and follow-up pack update.
Make the gate nonzero when a block rule fails.

resources.title: AI Agent Pilot Scorecard
resources.description: A scoring rubric for pilots where leaders need to compare agents on usefulness, reliability, cost, latency, and auditability.
resources.audience: AI platform teams and procurement reviewers

sections.title: Score Dimensions
items: Correct final outcome and task completion.
Tool selection quality and recovery after failed calls.
Cost per successful task, not raw token spend.

sections.title: Reliability Signals
items: Repeated runs on the same pack show stable behavior.
Failures cluster into named categories with owners.
Candidate keeps quality when inputs include messy real-world details.

sections.title: Evidence Quality
items: Replay explains the path from prompt to final answer.
Artifacts are downloadable and tied to the scorecard.
Security can review tool paths without reading raw logs for hours.

sections.title: Pilot Decision
items: Ship, extend pilot, or reject with the exact failed dimension.
Name the next workload to freeze if the pilot advances.
Turn pilot misses into regression coverage before expansion.

resources.title: Agent Procurement Questions
resources.kicker: Buyer checklist for AI agent platforms
resources.description: A focused list of questions procurement and security teams can use when vendors claim their agents are production ready.

sections.title: Evaluation Method
items: Can we run the agent on our own challenge pack and private data?
Can we compare it against our current baseline under identical tools?
Can we repeat the same run after model, prompt, or harness changes?

sections.title: Evidence Access
items: Do we get replay, artifacts, and scorecards for failed cases?
Which evidence is hidden because the vendor hosts the agent?
Can audit teams export redacted evidence for committee review?

sections.title: Control And Policy
items: Can tool permissions, network access, and secrets be scoped per workload?
Can cost and latency limits block a release automatically?
Can failures become permanent regression tests?

sections.title: Commercial Fit
items: Are LLM tokens marked up or bring-your-own-key?
What happens to replay retention after the pilot?
Can we self-host or use dedicated infrastructure if required?

resources.title: 30-Day Agent Eval Rollout Plan
resources.kicker: Adoption plan for first release gate
resources.description: A practical rollout calendar for going from ad hoc agent testing to a baseline, challenge pack, and first CI gate.

sections.title: Week 1: Pick The Wedge
items: Choose one workflow with visible business impact and recurring failures.
Collect 20 to 50 real examples from tickets, repos, support cases, or operations logs.
Name the release owner and review committee.

sections.title: Week 2: Freeze The Pack
items: Turn examples into a versioned challenge pack with expected outputs and scorers.
Record the current production baseline.
Agree on correctness, cost, latency, and evidence expectations.

sections.title: Week 3: Compare Candidates
items: Run baseline, candidate, and any vendor agent under the same constraints.
Review replay for every critical fail.
Update scorers where the committee finds ambiguous evidence.

sections.title: Week 4: Gate The Release
items: Commit the gate manifest and wire it into CI.
Publish the first decision readout with links to scorecards and replay.
Schedule the first regression promotion review.
